meaning: 譛ｨ蟆泌擱[蟾ｴ蝓ｺ譁ｯ蝮ｦ荳ｭ荳憺Κ蝓主ｸ﹅
examples: Multan is also known for its many Sufi shrines, like the 14th-century, white-domed tomb in the background.
Dancing horses are trained by Faizal Abbas, a feudal landlord who pursues the hobby at his compound near Multan.
We arrive in Multan surrounded by a dozen jeeps. We are further north and apparently in the most dangerous area of our trip.
Speaking to reporters at the family's home in Multan, the elder Gilani appeared shaken but composed.
Three attackers were said to have come from the central Pakistani city of Multan, Mr Maria said.
